en: Based on your Sustainability and ESG responses, you are required to report your GHG emissions on a consistent basis. This will provide visibility to your connected Clients to see relevant reporting of Scope 1 and 2 emissions.\n\nPlease report the total MT CO2e for that scope and period in your responses below.  Ensure that your inputs align with either a monthly or quarterly input, based on your GHG reporting frequency.
Fuel burned in buildings or equipment your organization owns or operates. Include building heating fuels, like oil or gas, fuel used in stationary generators, and fuel-powered production equipment.
Do you track and calculate GHG for Stationary combustion?
Please provide your calculated quarterly MT CO2e for Stationary combustion
The production and distribution of electricity you purchase from the utility or another supplier. If your electricity mix is high in fossil fuels, your emissions will be higher.
Do you track and calculate GHG for Purchased electricity?
Please provide your calculated quarterly MT CO2e for Purchased electricity

Steam, heat, and cooling generated off-site that you purchase from a utility or another supplier—for instance, district heating and cooling, or steam used in industrial processes. Not to be confused with heat you generate on-site by using a boiler or furnace or cooling your facility with an electricity-powered AC unit.
Do you track and calculate GHG for Purchased steam, heat or cooling?
Please provide your calculated quarterly MT CO2e for Purchased steam, heat and cooling
